label: defines (X defines Y; defining scope -> defined construct)
gloss: Scope X directly introduces named construct Y as an immediate child of its body.
notes: Direct (one-step) definition edge; each defined construct has exactly one direct definer by construction of the lexical tree. Structural (non-NSM) definition.

definition.definition: defines(X, Y), forward direction = defining scope -> defined construct. Scope X (a python-module body, a python-class body, or a python-function body) directly introduces named construct Y (a python-function or a python-class) as an immediate child statement of X's body. The edge is one lexical step only; every defined construct has exactly one direct definer.
